title: Revision 1: Caesar Cipher mechanics & Rail Fence Cipher Patterns
body:
You've already learned how the Caesar Cipher shifts letters forwards or backwards in the alphabet to encode and decode messages. In this revision lesson, you'll apply those ideas more confidently: reversing a shift to decode, and applying a shift to encode. 
Focus on keeping track of the alphabet's circular movement — when shifts go past A or Z, the letters wrap around. Your goal is simply to apply the correct direction and amount of shift to each letter. Try to keep track of letter mappings to make encoding/decoding longer messages easier.
lesson: CRYPTO-D1A-9f5a2f70, CRYPTO-D1B-55c1a1de, CRYPTO-D4A-8b7f12c9, CRYPTO-D4B-74ad0c21, CRYPTO-D5A-90c3e4e2, CRYPTO-D5B-1f0a76bd
